extraction.one_line_summary: BILBAO Pello attacked from 25-rider breakaway, held 21 seconds over chasing THOMAS group on final climb to La Rosière
tactical_patterns: AG2R sent LATOUR ahead after pacing, then he bridged to breakaway—dual role pre-positioning for final climb.
SKY's THOMAS waited until final kilometer to attack for bonus seconds despite wearing yellow, showing GC aggression.
Breakaway of 25 survived because AG2R refused to chase their own rider LATOUR after summit three.

rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: positive: soloed from quality breakaway on final climb, suggesting underrated climbing endurance in multi-stage mountain races
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: attacked in final kilometer while in yellow to gain bonus seconds, tactically sharper than passive race leadership
rider_signals.LATOUR Pierre: positive: bridged from peloton to breakaway post-summit after pacing, rare successful transition showing excellent recovery
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: dropped on Col de la Prey by AG2R tempo, post-Giro fatigue confirmed as handicapping factor
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: attacked halfway up final climb strong enough to split favorites, more aggressive than brother's typical racing
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: positive: fourth place from domestique role shows form building, AG2R tactics prioritized stage over his GC
